text.title: Cloud Run Monitoring Failure Modes
text.summary: 4 antipatterns commonly observed when applying the cloud-run-monitoring methodology. Each pairs a symptom + root-cause + concrete fix.

antipattern.symptom: Cloud Logging cannot filter / aggregate; investigation is full-text search.
antipattern.root-cause: Application emits `print(...)` or `logger.info(plain text)` to stdout.
antipattern.fix: Switch to JSON formatter writing severity + message + trace; verify Cloud Logging shows structured fields.

antipattern.symptom: Production incidents are detected by user reports, not by monitoring.
antipattern.root-cause: `gcloud alpha monitoring policies list --filter='display_name~service-name'` returns empty.
antipattern.fix: Create alerts on 5xx rate, p99 latency, instance count, CPU utilisation.

antipattern.symptom: Cross-service traces fracture; root cause analysis blocked across microservices.
antipattern.fix: Wrap HTTP client to propagate trace header; verify Cloud Trace shows connected spans.

antipattern.symptom: Logged credentials leak to BigQuery sinks and broadly-readable buckets.
antipattern.root-cause: Log entries contain `password=`, `token=`, or PII patterns (grep on log sink).
antipattern.fix: Add redaction at logger level; review last 30 days of logs and rotate any leaked credentials.
